human_verification.notes: Reviewed the housing finance specialist package for HUD, eCFR, IRS, federal award, and internal-control source quality; billing, posting, reconciliation, denial, draw, and unsupported adjustment coverage; authority boundaries; functionality-map completeness; deployment posture; and validated spec-pack consistency.

notes: Shared abilities, revenue-billing-and-financial-reconciliation abilities, and cross-industry artifact awareness are all mapped to passing scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in functionality-map scenario coverage.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to housing finance billing readiness, posting support, reconciliation, exception escalation, and source traceability.

Authority sources cover HUD multifamily financial operations, HUD occupancy and rental assistance records, TRACS voucher workflow, HCV/HAP payment rules, HOME disbursement and recordkeeping, FASS reporting, FHA multifamily development processing, LIHTC noncompliance evidence, federal award controls, and internal-control standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal HAP reconciliation, unsupported rent or HAP adjustment, TRACS denial, unsupported write-off and residual receipts override, missing HOME draw evidence, occupancy and legal boundary routing, missing funding stack and systems, housing terminology normalization, source conflict, tenant adaptation, LIHTC adjacent routing, and development draw reconciliation without approval.

Research summary maps every cross-industry domain scenario family and queue-level must-pass family to passing scenarios and converts property, funding-stack, source-system, approver, lender, accounting-policy, and tax variation into tenant-required facts or escalation rules.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around rent-setting, subsidy eligibility, HAP payment approval, HOME draw approval, development draw approval, cost certification, LIHTC tax treatment, accounting policy, write-off approval, owner distributions, legal interpretation, lender approval, audit signoff, and financial statement certification.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated housing finance specialist for source-backed housing billing, posting, reconciliation, and exception handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
